summary: Question 1.12 is resolved negatively within the same paper: the authors provide an explicit counterexample for every k >= 3 (a cycle on 2k-1 vertices with one additional vertex adjacent to two consecutive cycle vertices), showing that two disjoint P_k need not contain two disjoint avoidable P_k. The cases k in {1,2} have positive answers by results of Beisegel et al. A web search found no follow-up papers further studying this specific question.
search_queries: avoidable paths disjoint graphs Bonamy Defrain Hatzel Thiebaut 1908.03788
avoidability beyond paths disjoint P_k counterexample 2208.12803
notes: The counterexample is given within the source paper itself (not in a follow-up), so there are no since_posted entries. The related paper 'Avoidability beyond paths' (arXiv:2208.12803, Gurvich, Krnc, Milanič, 2022) extends avoidability to two-rooted graphs but does not directly address Question 1.12 about disjoint paths.
